Class Position

java.lang.Object
ca.sfu.cmpt276.group8.Position
public class Position extends Object
Represents a position in a 2D grid using row and column coordinates.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Position(int row, int col)
    Constructs a Position with the specified row and column.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    equals(Object obj)
    Compares this Position to another object for equality.
    int
    getCol()
    Returns the column coordinate of this position.
    int
    getRow()
    Returns the row coordinate of this position.
    int
    hashCode()
    Returns a hash code value for this Position.
    void
    setCol(int col)
    Sets the column coordinate of this position.
    void
    setRow(int row)
    Sets the row coordinate of this position.
    String
    toString()
    Returns a string representation of this position in the form "(row, col)".

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Position

      public Position(int row, int col)
      Constructs a Position with the specified row and column.
      Parameters:
      row - the row coordinate
      col - the column coordinate

  • Method Details

    • getRow

      public int getRow()
      Returns the row coordinate of this position.
      Returns:
      the row

    • getCol

      public int getCol()
      Returns the column coordinate of this position.
      Returns:
      the column

    • setRow

      public void setRow(int row)
      Sets the row coordinate of this position.
      Parameters:
      row - the new row

    • setCol

      public void setCol(int col)
      Sets the column coordinate of this position.
      Parameters:
      col - the new column

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this position in the form "(row, col)".
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      the string representation

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this Position to another object for equality.

      Two Position objects are considered equal if they have the same row and column values. This method allows logical comparison of Position instances rather than relying on default reference equality.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the object to compare with this Position
      Returns:
      true if the given object is a Position with the same row and column; false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this Position.

      The hash code is computed based on the row and column values.

      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code value for this Position
